citations.quote: The accommodation support service can: ... Provide a landlord reference within 5 working days when you email us at accommodation@napier.ac.uk
ENSA Advice ... Advice on tenant rights and responsibilities ... Help with disputes (landlord, flatmates, deposit)
We operate a waiting list for University accommodation after our guarantee date in August.
Please note that being placed on the waiting list does not guarantee a place in university accommodation

findings.text: The brochure describes a private accommodation support service that can provide a landlord reference within 5 working days.
The brochure signposts ENSA Advice for tenant rights, lease review, and dispute help.
The brochure mentions a university accommodation waiting list after August but states it does not guarantee a place.
The brochure contains no specific policies on rent arrears, late payment, eviction, or financial penalties. Its relevance to an arrears complaint is limited to contextual support services.

proposed_tasks.instructions: Search the matter source inventory for any tenancy agreement, licence to occupy, university accommodation policy, or arrears/rent collection policy documents. If none found, propose a task to request these from the client or university. The current brochure (NAP-SRC-0014) does not contain arrears procedures.
proposed_tasks.title: Locate tenancy agreement and arrears policy documents

summary: Reviewed NAP-SRC-0014 (Private Accommodation Brochure 2025) for relevance to Anfal's accommodation arrears complaint. The brochure describes university support services, flat-finding guidance, and tenant rights signposting. It does not contain any specific policy on arrears, rent payment deadlines, late fees, or eviction procedures. Relevance is limited to contextual background on available advice services. No contradictions, redaction, or risk flags identified. Proposed follow-up task to locate actual tenancy or arrears policy documents.

uncertainties.text: No arrears, rent payment, or eviction policy found in this brochure. Actual tenancy terms or university arrears procedures are not present.
